steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The farmers have no access to inorganic soil additives, so the correct amendment must be organic only. The table shows fruit weight, fruit length, and fruit diameter for each treatment; among the organic options, the best choice produces the largest gains in all three measurements.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — biochar and NPK fertilizer: NPK fertilizer is inorganic — excluded by the constraint. ✗
  • Choice B — biochar and compost: both organic; produces 979.3 g weight (153.4 g above the 825.9 g control), 6.76 cm fruit length (0.62 cm above control), and 13.68 cm fruit diameter (0.05 cm above control) — the largest gains across all three measurements among organic options. ✓
  • Choice C — biochar alone: organic, but weight gain is only 89.8 g above control — far less than choice B. ✗
  • Choice D — compost alone: organic, but weight gain is only 38.9 g above control — the smallest improvement among organic choices. ✗
That is the correct answer.
